cases.output:
Files written to `/tmp/contact-form/` — open `index.html` in any browser to see it (no build step, no server needed).

**Design choices & why**:
- **Typography**: Instrument Serif (italic accent word) + Manrope body — deliberately avoiding the Inter/Space Grotesk/Poppins cliché. The italic `something` in the heading is the signature element.
- **Palette**: warm off-white `#F4F1EC` with a single terracotta accent `#B85C38`, instead of the stock white + purple gradient pattern.
- **Texture**: inline SVG fractal noise at ~7% opacity — gives the paper-like warmth you asked for without an image asset.
- **Layout**: asymmetric 2-column (1.05fr / 1fr) on ≥860px, stacks cleanly on mobile.
- **Form styling**: underline-only inputs (no boxy fields) for a minimal editorial feel; the submit arrow slides on hover.
- **A11y**: semantic `<main>`/`<section>`, labels on every field, `:focus-visible` outlines, `aria-live` status region, `prefers-reduced-motion` respected, `novalidate` so JS owns validation UX.
- **Submission stub**: `setTimeout` fake — swap the try block in `script.js` for `fetch()` to Formspree / Netlify / your handler.

**What I could NOT verify** (no browser tools available in this session — you'll want to eyeball these):
1. Google Fonts actually load (Instrument Serif + Manrope) — if they don't, check network tab.
2. The SVG grain texture renders visibly but subtly on your display.
3. Responsive breakpoint at 860px behaves (resize the window through that width).
4. Submit button hover arrow animation feels right.
5. Validation error states look good (try submitting empty, then with bad email).

**Note on scope**: this is unrelated to the active `0670-skill-builder-universal` increment in the vskill repo — I kept it in `/tmp/` so it doesn't pollute the repo. Move the three files into your portfolio project when you're happy with them.

The design choices emphasize minimalism and avoid overloading with effects, satisfying the assertion.
